data.title: SL Green refinances corporate credit facility
data.description:
SL Green Realty (SLG) has refinanced, extended, and reduced the overall cost of $2B of its $2.4B corporate credit facility.

The existing revolving line of credit component of the facility has been maintained at $1.25B, the maturity date has been extended to June 2031, inclusive of as-of-right extension options, and the borrowing cost was reduced by 25 basis points to 125 basis points over SOFR based on the company’s current credit rating.

The existing $1.05B term loan component of the facility has been bifurcated, resulting in a new $750M term loan with a maturity date of June 2031 and a borrowing cost that has been reduced by 25 basis points to 145 basis points over SOFR, based on the company’s current credit rating. The remaining $300M of the term loan with a maturity date of May 2027 will continue to be outstanding on its current terms.

The existing $100M term loan component of the facility with a maturity date of November 2026 will also remain outstanding on its current terms.

“The refinancing of our credit facility is another meaningful step forward in the execution of our $7.0 billion 2026 financing plan. The strength of the Midtown Manhattan office leasing market, coupled with the credit quality of our portfolio and our platform, continues to attract the support of the world’s highest quality financial institutions,” said Matt DiLiberto, Chief Financial Officer of SL Green.

data.title: i-80 Gold prices upsized offering of $250 million convertible senior notes
data.description:
• i-80 Gold (IAUX) has priced its offering of unsecured convertible senior notes due 2031. The company intends to issue $250 million aggregate principal amount of notes 
  • The notes will bear cash interest paid semi-annually at 3.75% per annum. The initial conversion rate for the notes will be 519.4805 common shares of the company per $1,000 principal amount of notes, equivalent to an initial conversion price of ~$1.93 per share.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about March 23, 2026.
  • i-80 Gold intends to use the net proceeds from this offering to advance the company's gold projects through various stages of development, refurbish the Lone Tree processing plant, and fund resource expansion and infill drilling, as well as for general corporate and working capital purposes.
  • IAUX -3.57% premarket to $1.35.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Pfizer posts late-stage trial win for Talzenna plus Xtandi in new prostate cancer indication
data.description:
Pfizer (PFE) announced on Thursday that its cancer drug regimen, Talzenna plus Xtandi, met the primary endpoint in a Phase 3 trial for a new indication in prostate cancer.

Xtandi, marketed by Pfizer (PFE) with Astellas (ALPMF) (ALPMY), is currently available in the U.S. in combination with Talzenna, a PARP inhibitor, for adults with HRR gene-mutated metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC).

The Phase 3 TALAPRO-3 study was designed to evaluate Talzenna plus Xtandi in HRR gene-mutated metastatic castration-sensitive prostate cancer ((mCSPC)) also identified as metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer.

Citing topline data, the New York-based pharma giant said that TALAPRO-3 met the primary goal, as patients on Talzenna plus Xtandi experienced a statistically significant and clinically meaningful improvement in radiographic progression-free survival compared to those on placebo plus Xtandi.

The interim analysis also indicated a favorable trend for overall survival ((OS)), a key secondary endpoint, in those who received the two-drug regimen.

According to the company, there were no new safety events, and the experimental therapy was associated with safety findings that were consistent with the established safety profile of each medicine.

Pfizer (PFE) expects to present TALAPRO-3 data at an upcoming medical event and discuss the findings with global health authorities seeking regulatory submissions.

data.title: Micron slides as investors take profits after historic run; analysts praise guidance
data.description:
Micron Technology (MU) was in focus on Thursday as Wall Street analysts praised the memory maker after it reported much stronger-than-expected results and guidance. However, investors took profits after a historic run.

Shares fell nearly 7% in premarket trading but have gained more than 350% over the past 12 months. Western Digital (WDC), Sandisk (SNDK), and Seagate Technology (STX), which often trade in tandem with Micron, also dipped in premarket trading.

Wells Fargo analyst Aaron Rakers said the results and guidance reinforced that there is a strong structural story to the memory market. “Our long-standing [positive] thesis on MU has been underpinned by the belief that the role memory plays in driving the continued evolution of AI infra build-outs will continually expand + deepen (DRAM + NAND / eSSDs),” Rakers wrote in a note to clients. “This is further bolstered by MU's SCA engagements.” He upped his price target on the stock to $550 from $470 and said the company could earn around $40 per share in the middle of the cycle.

Bank of America analyst Vivek Arya was of a similar mindset, as he too raised his price target on the stock, moving to $500 from $400. “Particularly, memory pricing could remain elevated for longer (albeit stabilize) given: memory is a critical driver of tokenomics; new 5-year supply contract (SCA) that is cross-cycle by nature, on top of traditional 1-year LTAs; and overall limited cleanroom space through ~CY27-28E.” 

Arya added that Micron could earn as much as $76.50 per share in fiscal 2027; however, there is a risk that spot pricing for memory starts to stabilize soon. He pointed out that spot pricing for DDR4 and DDR5 is now around $30 for 16 GB, compared to between $3 and $10 historically. 

“NAND spot continues to benefit from anticipated increase in DC products (eSSDs, NVDA KV cache offload, etc.), though per AMAT demand created from KV cache could only be single-digit % of overall NAND TAM,” Arya explained. “Similarly, MU FQ3 [gross margin] guide of 81.0% could be near peak cycle, eventually stabilizing toward 60-70% historical high prior to AI.”

RBC Capital Markets analyst Srini Pajjuri said the decline in the stock could be a concern about the peak margins and rising capex spending, but the outlook is extremely bright.

“On the demand front, GenAI remains strong and structural drivers (DC mix >50%, HBM >20% of DRAM industry) are playing a much larger role in the current upcycle,” Pajjuri wrote in a note to clients. “As such, we expect pricing to remain healthy into 2027 and see sizable multiple expansion potential as sustainability becomes apparent.”

data.title: Datavault AI GAAP EPS of -$0.52, revenue of $39.09M
data.description:
• Datavault AI press release (DVLT): FY GAAP EPS of -$0.52.
  • Revenue of $39.09M (+1364.0% Y/Y).

  • Reiterates FY-26 revenue target of $200 million, representing a nearly 400% YoY increase from FY-25

data.title: TG Therapeutics secures $750M credit facility with Blue Owl, boosts buyback to $300M
data.description:
• TG Therapeutics (TGTX) has entered into a new five-year, $750M senior secured credit facility with funds managed by Blue Owl Capital (OWL).
  • As part of the transaction, the company will repay its outstanding $250M senior secured credit facility, resulting in a net raise of $500M in non-dilutive capital. The new facility also provides for up to an additional $250M of incremental capital, for a total facility size of up to $1B, available at the mutual discretion of TG and Blue Owl.
  • In connection with the new facility, the company’s board of directors authorized an increase to its share repurchase program from $100M to $300M. As of March 18, 2026, the company has repurchased approximately $38M of common stock under the prior and existing share repurchase program at an average price of $28.98 per share
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Arcos Dorados Holdings Inc. GAAP EPS of $0.12 misses by $0.08, revenue of $1.27B beats by $10M
data.description:

data.title: Intuitive Machines reports Q4 results
data.description:
• Intuitive Machines press release (LUNR): Q4 Net loss of ~$40M
  • Revenue of $44.79M (-18.1% Y/Y) misses by $14.21M.
  • Combined Company February month end backlog of approximately $943 million

  • 2026 Outlook

    • Full-year 2026 revenue of $900 million - $1 billion (vs. consensus of $899.98M)
    • Full-year 2026 Adjusted EBITDA positive

data.title: Global recorded music revenue hit $31.7B in 2025 as paid streaming surged
data.description:
According to a report by the International Federation of the Phonographic Industry, the Swiss not-for-profit group that represents the global recording industry, revenue from recorded music worldwide rose 6.4% to $31.7B in 2025, as more people subscribed to paid music services.

The report noted that revenue from paid streaming platforms grew by 8.8% and made up more than half of global revenue. 

When looking at revenue growth by region, Asia, MENA, Sub-Saharan Africa, and Latin America posted double-digit percentage gains, with LATAM growing the fastest at 17.1% from last year.

Music revenue from the U.S. and Canada, the world’s largest recorded music region, rose 3.5%. The region accounted for a 38.7% share of global revenue.

Europe retained its spot as the second-largest region and grew revenue by 5.6%; Australasia was up 1.5%.

The IFPI report indicated that music revenue from physical formats saw an 8% growth, which includes a near 14% rise in vinyl purchases. Performance rights revenue grew for a fifth consecutive year, albeit marginally, to $2.9B.

The report highlighted that with the ongoing advancement in artificial intelligence, record companies are poised to take advantage of the innovation.

"Music is embracing the future, demonstrated by record company partnerships with generative AI developers who respect the rights of creators," IFPI chief executive Victoria Oakley said. "They are partners that explore how technology can be harnessed to support and enhance creativity, not replace it."

IFPI also flagged concerns about the music industry being exposed to the risk of streaming fraud, which could erode away revenue from artists.

"Streaming fraud is theft, plain and simple," Oakley said. "The organizations with the data, scale, and leverage to prevent this fraudulent activity, including streaming services, content aggregators, and distributors, must take decisive action."

data.title: Klarna loses several senior executives, including investor relations head
data.description:
Klarna Group (KLAR), the buy now, pay later lender that went public in September, lost several  high-level executives recently, including its head of investor relations and M&A and its global head of litigation.

The company's head of investor relations and M&A, Andrea Ferraz Estrada, left in March after six years with Klarna (KLAR), and D. Andrew Pietro, global head of litigation, left the company after a three-year tenure, their LinkedIn profiles said.

Ferraz joined Delivery Hero as vice president of investor relations and corporate communications, while Pietro became head of litigation at Greystar.

Last month, Yuri Gusev, engineering director, left after eight years at the company. Joao Tonon, head of AI and automation, departed in January, his LinkedIn post said.

The departures come amid a sharp decline in the company's stock. It has dropped 67% from its $40 IPO price since it started trading on Sept. 9, 2025. On March 13, the stock gained 8.8% after Klarna (KLAR) chairman Michael Moritz bought 3.47M shares for ~$50M worth.

IPO stocks have hit a rough patch amid volatile equity markets buffeted by economic uncertainty over tariffs and the recent Iran war. The Renaissance IPO ETF (IPO) has slid 18% in the past six months.
